operating_state: Net revenues declined 5% to $12.22 billion driven by significantly lower investment banking fees from reduced M&A and debt underwriting volumes, and 10% lower market making revenues despite solid activity levels. Global Banking & Markets revenues fell with FICC intermediation declining in currencies and commodities, partially offset by higher interest rate products. Asset & Wealth Management revenues increased from NNIP inclusion and higher management fees, while Platform Solutions revenues more than doubled driven by Consumer platforms growth.
cost_structure: Operating expenses increased 9% to $8.40 billion primarily from real estate investment impairments and NNIP inclusion, driving efficiency ratio to 68.7% versus 59.7% prior year. Compensation remained flat including severance costs from headcount reduction initiatives that reduced workforce 6%. The G-SIB surcharge increased from 2.5% to 3.0% effective January 2023, raising the Standardized CET1 capital ratio requirement to 13.8%.
material_events: Executed partial sale of Marcus loans portfolio with remainder transferred to held for sale, generating approximately $470 million loss largely offset by reserve reduction. Returned $3.41 billion to shareholders through share repurchases and dividends, with Board approving new $30 billion share repurchase program. Participated in consortium deposit to support First Republic Bank during March banking sector stress.
macro_exposures: Banking sector stress including regional bank failures created interest rate volatility with 2-year Treasury yields moving over 15 basis points daily for seven consecutive days in March. Recorded provision for credit losses primarily related to First Republic exposure reflecting banking sector contagion risks. Interest rate risk exposure increased significantly with Earnings-at-Risk sensitivity rising to $221 million gain for 100 basis point parallel rate increases from $104 million in December 2022.
forward_outlook: Reaffirmed through-the-cycle targets of 14-16% ROE, 15-17% ROTE, and approximately 60% efficiency ratio with expected 2023 tax rate between 21-22%. Management targets firmwide management fees exceeding $10 billion including alternatives by 2024, with Asset & Wealth Management targeting mid-twenties pre-tax margins within three to five years. Platform Solutions targets sub-100% efficiency ratio in 2023 and pre-tax profitability by end-2025.
uncertainty_profile: Consumer platforms reporting unit goodwill remains at risk given fair value proximity to carrying value and potential GreenSky sale implications. Allowance for credit losses modeling incorporates weighted economic scenarios with adverse weighting potentially requiring additional provision. The firm awaits FDIC rulemaking on special assessments for bank failures which could materially impact results depending on final assessment application.
